Run by college students, high-school students & coaches with a decade of experience · students raising funds for local schools & educational causes.
About us

Who are we (Yogic Transformers)?

A community STEAM program in Ashburn, VA — run by students, for students, with experienced coaches alongside.

Meet the masterminds behind your camper's summer: a league of extraordinary high school and college friends who spent the last decade conquering World Robotics stages, crushing STEM Olympiads, and coaching over 400 future innovators—all while our family collectively raised over $200K (of that our boys raised $40k) for educational causes. When the tight job market threw them a curveball this summer, they did what they do best: joined forces to transform obstacles into opportunities!

The result? The ultimate, budget-friendly, hands-on STEAM playground featuring customizable, weekly themed camps where kids can mix and match their adventures. It's a massive win-win: elite, world-class mentorship for your budget, and an unforgettable summer of explosive, creative fun for your kids. Your campers won't just learn STEAM—they'll catch the contagious energy of real-world makers who know exactly how to turn big dreams into reality. And maybe even start their journey into being a Yogic Transformer too!

Our people

Team & Instructors

Near-peer instructors who grew up in these programs, backed by veteran coaches and certified wellness teachers.

Coaches & mentors

Adult Coaches

LCPS parents with 10+ years leading STEAM camps — 400+ students taught. They design the curriculum, train instructors, and supervise every session.

Who teaches

Student Instructors

College and high-school students who came up through these programs and now teach them — relatable mentors who make hard ideas click.

Well-being

SKY Youth Teachers

Certified SKY Youth Teachers lead the Sudarshan Kriya (SKY) Kids & Teens programs, bringing breathing, focus, and life-skills practices to camp.

Meet our instructors

The teachers leading our STEAM Activity topics.

Namrata
Bollywood Dance · Instructor

Namrata has been dancing since age 5 and brings 15+ years of Bollywood dance experience, plus 8 years of formal Bharatanatyam training. At Virginia Tech she danced 3 years with the collegiate hip-hop team Dancing’s Our Poison, learning performance and contemporary choreography in a collaborative team environment.

@naachwithnamrata ↗ ► Performance videos ↗
Nidhi
Design & Creativity · Instructor

Nidhi is a sophomore at VCU majoring in psychology. During her senior year of high school she ran the design team for The Blaze, the RRHS high school newspaper, and earned a First-Class Award from VHSL. She teaches design skills using Adobe, and has pursued art as a hobby her whole life — she’s well known as an artistic person by all who know her!

The Blaze ↗
Nishitha
Design & Creativity · Instructor

Nishitha is a sophomore at Virginia Tech majoring in Data Science. She has 8 years of experience working with students in grades K–12 through volunteering, including 3 years at Kumon.

Rithika
Design & Creativity · Instructor

Rithika is an incoming freshman at Case Western Reserve University and a graduate of Briar Woods High School. She worked at Goldfish Swim School for a year as a swim instructor and lifeguard, and volunteered at summer camps at Chinmaya Somnath for 3 years. She is certified as an EMT, lifeguard, and fencing referee, and — besides English — is proficient in German. She has worked with the Art of Living in many areas, from helping prepare meals to supporting children’s programs. Rithika also enjoys spending time with friends and family, baking, reading, and exploring new sports and activities, and is passionate about helping others and deepening her understanding of science and healthcare. She spent two years on her school’s Science Olympiad team, competing across a variety of subjects.

Anika
Design & Creativity · Instructor

Anika is a rising senior at Rock Ridge High School. She has been passionate about art for years and is largely self-taught — and because of that, she’s confident in her ability to teach what she’s learned to others. She also has plenty of experience tutoring, both younger kids and high schoolers.

Anwita
Business & Public Speaking · Instructor

Anwita is a rising sophomore at Rock Ridge High School. Through DECA she placed first in her category at districts and advanced to states, and her time on the school debate team helped her build strong speaking skills. She enjoys teaching and collaborating with students on topics she’s passionate about, like public speaking and business, and competed in FLL during her middle school years among many other competitions.

Dr. Nilakshi Deshpande
Design & Creativity · Instructor

Dr. Nilakshi Deshpande holds a Ph.D. in botany and microbiology and has published several research papers. She also has extensive experience teaching and practicing fine arts, working with students through local after-school programs and at her home for over 8 years.

Hemal Mehta
Design & Creativity · Instructor

Hemal has run Hemal Art Studio in Ashburn for the past 6 years and brings extensive experience in teaching and practicing fine arts, having taught many students and adults over the years.

The program

What is the STEAM Camp about?

Hands-on building across six STEAM areas — plus well-being and optional competition prep.

Every session is project-driven. Students build robots, code games and apps, engineer and 3D-print designs, create art, and grow as leaders and communicators — then learn to slow down and focus.

Work spans six areas: Robotics, Programming, Engineering, Design & Creativity, Leadership & Communication, and Competition Prep (TSA and Science Olympiad, where students pick their own event). Browse the full catalog in Activity Topics below.

Robotics Programming Engineering Design & Creativity Leadership & Comm. Competition Prep

Woven throughout: a Sudarshan Kriya (SKY) Youth Program for focus and well-being.

Our purpose

Why are we conducting this camp?

Great learning, real well-being, and a give-back to the community.

Kids learn STEAM best from people only a few years ahead of them. We put students in front of the room and experienced adults beside them, so curiosity stays high and support stays close.

Technical skill isn't enough on its own. We pair building and competing with well-being — focus, breathing, and life skills — so students leave more capable and calmer.

And it's a give-back: the students teaching here are raising funds for local schools and non-profit causes, so every registration sends help back into the community.

Give-back

How are we paying it forward?

You choose where a portion of the proceeds goes.

🤝

A portion of every registration (after expenses) is donated to local schools and educational non-profit causes — and you decide where it goes.

During registration, name the school or educational cause you'd like your child's contribution directed toward. The students teaching here are raising those funds, so learning and giving happen in the same summer.
